title: wyethia sagittata
intro: Wyethia sagittata, also known as milkweed or mule's ears, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the western United States. The medicinal properties of Wyethia sagittata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant has several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Wyethia sagittata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used to make topical creams and salves for skin conditions.
